steps.title: Spot the explanatory relationship
steps.content: The first clause introduces a concept: "The visual magnitude scale is reverse logarithmic." The second clause must clarify what that concept means. The transition needs to signal this clarification.

steps.title: Apply the clarifying transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is specification/clarification: "reverse logarithmic" is a technical term, and the second clause explains what it means in plain terms (brighter = lower number). The blank needs a transition signaling restatement or explanation.
  • Choice A — "similarly,": signals that a comparable situation follows, so fails — the second clause is not a parallel example but a definition of the first clause. ✗
  • Choice B — "in contrast,": signals opposition, so fails — the second clause does not oppose the first; it defines it. ✗
  • Choice C — "in other words,": signals restatement or clarification of the same idea, so fits — the plain-language explanation of "reverse logarithmic" is exactly what follows. ✓
  • Choice D — "for example,": signals a specific instance of a general claim, so fails — the second clause defines the scale, it does not give one example from among many. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
